summary: High‑energy 1981 show featuring a smoking Wheel and the rare Let It Grow → China → I Know You Rider sequence; soundboard source is generally good with occasional tinny coloration.
review: Fans rave about this summer‑tour highlight, calling the second‑set Wheel one of the best of the ’80s and praising the first‑time appearance of Never Trust a Woman plus an epic Let It Grow → China → Rider run. The soundboard recording is mostly clear and lively, though a few listeners note a slightly tinny texture in places.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (10 out of 12) are highly positive about both performance and recording; only one reviewer notes a tinny sound and another gives a low rating, so the overall impression is a strong show with solid but not flawless audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and energetic; several fans call it an outstanding 1981 soundboard. One reviewer mentions a tinny, somewhat insubstantial character, but most find the mix clear and well‑balanced.

standout_songs: Let It Grow > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set that famously ends with Let It Grow → China Cat → I Know You Rider, followed by an extended Shakedown Street and a second‑set Wheel jam. The concert is packed with long jams and rare moments (first Never Trust a Woman), giving it a seamless, high‑energy flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring vibrola tone, “shot out of a cannon” guitar work, and overall stellar performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s blues‑tune receives mixed reactions (buzzkill vs. favorite version) but his solo on Big River is highlighted positively.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum/space segment (Spanish Jam) and overall drumming are called the best of any 1981 show.
